丸い標識に「30」と書かれている場合、それはその区間における最高速度を表している: When a round sign says ``30,'' it represents the maximum speed for that section.
赤い円に数字が書かれた標識は最高速度を示し、その数字を超える速度で走行してはなら: A sign with a number written in a red circle indicates the maximum speed, and you must not drive faster than that number.
三角形で赤い縁取りの標識は、警戒標識であり前方の危険を予告している。: A triangular sign with a red border is a warning sign and warns of danger ahead.
黄色地に赤枠の三角標識は警戒標識で、カーブや踏切など前方の危険な状況を運転者に知: A triangular sign with a red frame on a yellow background is a warning sign that alerts drivers to dangerous situations ahead, such as curves and railroad crossings.
「一方通行」の標識がある道路では、矢印で示された方向以外への進行は禁止されている: On roads with "one-way" signs, it is prohibited to proceed in any direction other than the direction indicated by the arrow.
一方通行の標識は青地に白矢印で進行方向を指定し、逆走は重大な交通違反となる。: One-way traffic signs indicate the direction of travel with a white arrow on a blue background, and driving in the wrong direction is a serious traffic violation.
「徐行」の標識がある場所では、車はすぐに停止できる速度まで落とさなければならない: Where there is a "slow down" sign, vehicles must reduce their speed to a point where they can stop immediately.
徐行とは直ちに停止できる速度のことであり、おおむね時速10km以下を意味する。: Slow speed refers to the speed at which you can stop immediately, and generally means 10km/h or less.
「止まれ」の標識がある交差点では、車は一時停止線で完全に停止する必要はなく減速す: At intersections with "stop" signs, cars do not need to come to a complete stop at the stop line; they only need to slow down.
「止まれ」の標識では停止線の直前で完全に停止し、安全を確認してから進行しなければ: At a "stop" sign, you must come to a complete stop just before the stop line and proceed after making sure it is safe.
「車両通行止め」と「車両進入禁止」は同じ意味であり、どちらも全車両の進入を禁止す: "Closed to vehicles" and "vehicles prohibited" have the same meaning, and both prohibit all vehicles from entering.
「車両通行止め」は両方向からの進入を禁止するが、「車両進入禁止」は一方向からの進: "Closed to Vehicles" is a sign that prohibits entry from both directions, but "No Vehicles Entry" is a sign that prohibits entry from only one direction.
「駐車禁止」の標識がある道路でも、5分以内であれば荷物の積み下ろしのために停車す: Even on roads with "no parking" signs, you can stop for up to 5 minutes to load or unload your luggage.
駐車禁止は長時間の駐車を禁じるものであり、運転者がすぐ移動できる短時間の停車（5: The parking ban prohibits long-term parking, but short-term stops that allow the driver to move on quickly (such as loading and unloading luggage within 5 minutes) are permitted.
「優先道路」の標識がある道路を走る車は、交差する道路の車に対して進路を譲る必要が: Vehicles driving on roads with "priority road" signs do not have to yield to vehicles on intersecting roads.
優先道路を走行する車は、交差する一般道路から進入する車に対して優先権を持つため、: Vehicles driving on priority roads have the right of way over vehicles approaching from intersecting general roads, so there is no obligation to yield to them.
補助標識に「大型車等」と表示されている規制標識は、普通自動車には適用されない。: Regulation signs that display "Large Vehicles, etc." on the auxiliary sign do not apply to regular vehicles.
補助標識で対象車種が限定されている場合、その規制はその車種にのみ適用され、普通自: If a supplementary sign restricts the applicable vehicle types, the regulation applies only to that vehicle type and does not apply to regular cars.
「指定方向外進行禁止」の標識で右折の矢印のみが示されている場合、その地点では右折: If a sign that says ``No proceeding outside the designated direction'' only shows a right turn arrow, no other way than a right turn can be made at that point.
矢印で示された方向のみ進行が許可され、それ以外の方向（直進や左折など）への進行は: Travel is only permitted in the direction indicated by the arrow, and travel in other directions (such as going straight or turning left) is prohibited.
信号機のない踏切の手前に設置されている「踏切あり」の警戒標識は、運転者に減速と注: ``Level Crossing'' warning signs are placed in front of railroad crossings without traffic lights to remind drivers to slow down and be careful.
踏切あり標識は前方に踏切が存在することを警告し、運転者は減速して安全を確認する必: A railroad crossing sign warns drivers that there is a railroad crossing ahead and requires them to slow down and check for safety.
「自動車専用」の標識がある道路は、原動機付自転車や軽車両も通行することができる。: Motorized bicycles and light vehicles can also pass on roads with "vehicles only" signs.
自動車専用の標識がある道路には、原動機付自転車・軽車両・歩行者は通行できない。: Motorized bicycles, light vehicles, and pedestrians are not allowed to pass on roads marked with vehicle-only signs.
「横断歩道」の標識は、その地点に歩行者が道路を横断する場所があることを示している: A "crosswalk" sign indicates that there is a place for pedestrians to cross the road at that point.
横断歩道の標識がある場所では歩行者の横断に備え、付近を通過する際は速度を落として: Where there is a crosswalk sign, be prepared for pedestrians to cross and slow down and use caution when passing nearby.
「追越し禁止」の標識がある区間では、前車を追い越すことはできないが、進路変更だけ: You cannot pass the vehicle in front of you in areas where there is a "No Passing" sign, but there is no problem if you just change direction.
追越し禁止標識は前の車を追い越して進む行為を禁止するものであり、追い越しを伴わな: A no-passing sign prohibits the act of passing the vehicle in front of you, and changing lanes without overtaking is not subject to the regulation.
「最大幅」を示す標識がある道路では、表示された幅を超える車幅の車両は通行できない: On roads where there is a sign indicating ``maximum width,'' vehicles with a width exceeding the indicated width cannot pass.
最大幅の標識は道路や橋などの幅に制限がある場所に設置され、表示幅を超える車両の通: Maximum width signs are placed on roads, bridges, and other locations with limited width, and prohibit vehicles exceeding the indicated width from passing.
信号機のない交差点で道幅が同じくらいの場合、左方から来る車が右方から来る車より優: If the roads are the same width at an intersection without traffic lights, cars coming from the left will have priority over cars coming from the right.
道幅が同じ交差点では、左方から来る車が優先となり、右方から来る車は左方車に道を譲: At intersections where the roads are the same width, cars coming from the left have priority, and cars coming from the right must yield to cars on the left.
交差点で右折する車は、直進または左折する対向車よりも先に通行することができる。: Vehicles turning right at an intersection can pass ahead of oncoming vehicles going straight or turning left.
右折車は直進・左折する対向車の進行を妨げてはならず、対向車が先に通過するのを待た: Vehicles turning right must not obstruct the progress of oncoming vehicles going straight or turning left, and must wait for the oncoming vehicle to pass first.
緊急自動車が近づいてきたときは、交差点を避けて道路の左側に寄り一時停止しなければ: When an emergency vehicle approaches, you must avoid the intersection and stop on the left side of the road.
緊急自動車に進路を譲るため、交差点付近では交差点を避けて道路の左側に寄り一時停止: The general rule is to avoid intersections and stop on the left side of the road to give way to emergency vehicles.
環状交差点（ラウンドアバウト）では、すでに環道内を走行している車が進入してくる車: At roundabouts, vehicles already on the ring road have priority over incoming vehicles.
環状交差点では環道内を走行中の車両が優先され、進入する車はその通行を妨げないよう: At roundabouts, vehicles traveling on the ring road have priority, and incoming vehicles must stop or slow down to avoid blocking traffic.
横断歩道を渡ろうとしている歩行者がいる場合、車は必ず一時停止してその通行を妨げて: If a pedestrian is attempting to cross at a crosswalk, vehicles must stop and do not obstruct the pedestrian's passage.
横断歩道やその付近を横断しようとする歩行者がいるときは、車は一時停止して歩行者の: When a pedestrian attempts to cross at or near a crosswalk, vehicles must stop temporarily and do not obstruct the pedestrian's passage.
坂道で狭い道路ですれ違いが困難な場合、上りの車が下りの車に道を譲るのが原則である: When it is difficult to pass each other on a narrow, hilly road, the general rule is for cars going uphill to give way to cars going downhill.
坂道のすれ違いでは、上り坂を走る車は安全な場所に停止しやすいため、下りの車に道を: When passing each other on a slope, cars traveling uphill tend to stop in a safe place, so the basic rule is to give way to vehicles traveling downhill.
通行禁止の規制がない狭い道路と広い道路が交わる場合、広い道路を走る車が優先される: When a narrow road and a wide road intersect, where there are no traffic restrictions, cars on the wide road have priority.
幅の異なる道路が交わる交差点では、広い道路を走行する車が狭い道路から来る車より優: At intersections where roads of different widths intersect, vehicles traveling on the wide road have priority over vehicles coming from the narrow road.
路面電車が後方から接近しているとき、軌道内を走行する車は速やかに軌道外へ出なけれ: When a streetcar is approaching from behind, cars running on the tracks must quickly move off the tracks.
路面電車の軌道内を走行している車は、後方から路面電車が接近したときは速やかに軌道: Vehicles traveling on tram tracks must quickly move off the tracks and give way when a tram approaches from behind.
歩行者が横断中の道路に車を進入させる場合、歩行者よりも自車の通過を優先させてもよ: When a vehicle enters a road where a pedestrian is crossing, the vehicle may be given priority over the pedestrian.
歩行者の安全が最優先であり、車は横断中の歩行者の通行を妨げてはならない。: Pedestrian safety is paramount, and vehicles must not obstruct pedestrian traffic while crossing.
交差点で警察官が手信号で交通整理をしている場合、信号機の表示よりも警察官の指示が: When a police officer is directing traffic using hand signals at an intersection, the police officer's instructions take priority over the traffic light display.
警察官の手信号による指示は信号機の表示よりも優先され、運転者は警察官の指示に従わ: A police officer's hand signal instructions have priority over traffic lights, and drivers must follow the police officer's instructions.
標識による速度規制がない一般道路での普通自動車の最高速度は、法定で時速60キロメ: The legal maximum speed for regular cars on public roads without posted speed limits is 60 kilometers per hour.
標識等で特に指定がない一般道路では、普通自動車の法定最高速度は時速60キロメート: On ordinary roads, unless otherwise specified by signs, the legal maximum speed for ordinary vehicles is 60 kilometers per hour.
消火栓から3メートル以内の場所は、駐車だけでなく停車も禁止されている。: It is prohibited not only to park but also to park within 3 meters of a fire hydrant.
消火栓や消防用設備から3メートル以内は、緊急時の使用を妨げないよう駐停車が禁止さ: Parking or parking within 3 meters of fire hydrants and fire equipment is prohibited so as not to interfere with their use in emergencies.
「最高速度40キロ」の補助標識がついた標識のある道路では、雨天時には50キロまで: On roads marked with a "maximum speed limit of 40km/h', speeds may be increased to 50km/h in rainy weather.
指定された最高速度は天候にかかわらず守らなければならず、悪天候時はむしろ速度を落: The specified maximum speed must be maintained regardless of the weather, and speeds should be reduced in bad weather.
「駐停車禁止」の標識がある場所では、運転者が車を離れなくても短時間の荷物の積み下: Where there is a "No Parking" sign, stopping for short periods of time to load or unload cargo is also prohibited, even if the driver does not leave the vehicle.
駐停車禁止は駐車と停車の両方を禁止するため、荷物の積み下ろしのための短時間停止も: The parking ban prohibits both parking and stopping, so short stops for loading and unloading are also not allowed.
夜間、道路上に駐車する場合は、尾灯や駐車灯をつけなくても道路照明が十分明るければ: When parking on the road at night, there is no problem even if you do not turn on taillights or parking lights as long as the road lighting is bright enough.
夜間の駐停車時には、道路照明の有無にかかわらず尾灯や駐車灯をつけて他の車に存在を: When parking or stopping at night, it is necessary to turn on tail lights and parking lights to indicate your presence to other vehicles, regardless of whether there are road lights or not.
坂の頂上付近やトンネル内は、駐車だけでなく停車も法律で禁止されている。: By law, it is prohibited not only to park but also to stop near the top of a hill or inside a tunnel.
視界が悪く後続車が対応しにくい坂の頂上付近やトンネル内は、駐停車禁止場所として法: By law, parking is prohibited near the top of a hill or inside a tunnel, where visibility is poor and it is difficult for following vehicles to respond.
交差点の側端から5メートル以内の場所は、駐車および停車が禁止されている。: Parking or stopping is prohibited within 5 meters of the side edge of an intersection.
交差点付近での駐停車は見通しを悪くし危険なため、交差点の側端から5メートル以内は: Parking near an intersection is dangerous because it impairs visibility, so parking is prohibited within 5 meters from the edge of the intersection.
「最低速度50キロ」の標識がある道路では、故障などやむを得ない理由がなくてもそれ: On roads where there is a "minimum speed 50 km/h" sign, you can drive slower than that without any unavoidable reason such as a breakdown.
最低速度の標識がある区間では、やむを得ない理由がない限り、その速度より遅く走行し: In areas where there is a minimum speed sign, you must not drive slower than that speed unless there is a compelling reason to do so.
車庫や駐車場の出入口から3メートル以内の道路上の部分は、駐車が禁止されている。: Parking is prohibited on the road within 3 meters of the entrance to a garage or parking lot.
車庫等の出入口から3メートル以内に駐車すると車両の出入りを妨げるため、この区間は: If you park within 3 meters of the entrance to a garage, etc., it will prevent vehicles from entering and exiting, so this area is designated as a no-parking area.
バス停の標識から10メートル以内の場所は、バスの運行時間に関係なく常に駐停車が禁: Parking within 10 meters of a bus stop sign is prohibited at all times, regardless of bus operating hours.
バス停の駐停車禁止は運行時間中のみ適用される場合が多く、補助標識で示された時間帯: Parking restrictions at bus stops often apply only during bus service hours, so it is necessary to check the times indicated on supplementary signs.
高速道路の本線車道における普通自動車の最高速度は、特に標識がない限り100キロメ: The maximum speed for regular vehicles on the main road of an expressway is 100 km/h unless otherwise marked.
高速道路の本線では、標識による特別な指定がない場合、普通自動車の法定最高速度は時: On main highways, the legal maximum speed for ordinary vehicles is 100 km/h unless otherwise specified by signs.
高速道路の本線車道には最低速度の規制があり、特に標識がない場合は時速50キロメー: There is a minimum speed limit on the main lanes of expressways, which is 50 km/h unless there are special signs.
高速道路の本線では交通の流れを保つため最低速度が定められ、標識がない場合は時速5: Minimum speeds are set on main highways to keep traffic flowing, and where there are no signs, the maximum speed is 50 kilometers per hour.
高速道路に入るときは、加速車線を使って本線を走る車の速度に近づけてから合流するべ: When entering a freeway, you should use the acceleration lane to get closer to the speed of cars on the main road before merging.
本線合流時は加速車線で十分に速度を上げ、本線を走る車の流れに近い速度で合流するこ: When merging onto the main line, increase your speed sufficiently in the acceleration lane and merge at a speed similar to the flow of traffic on the main line to avoid dangers such as rear-end collisions.
高速道路を走行中にタイヤがパンクした場合、その場ですぐに停止して三角表示板を出さ: If your tire becomes flat while driving on the highway, there is no problem even if you stop immediately and do not put out the triangle sign.
高速道路上で停止する場合は、後続車に知らせるため三角表示板や非常点滅灯を必ず使用: When stopping on a highway, you must always use the triangular display board or emergency flashing lights to notify following vehicles.
高速道路では、原動機付自転車や125cc以下の自動二輪車は通行することができない: mopeds and motorcycles under 125cc are not allowed on expressways.
高速道路を通行できるのは小型二輪を除く一定排気量以上の自動二輪車と自動車のみで、: Only motorcycles and cars over a certain displacement, excluding small motorcycles, are allowed to travel on the expressway; mopeds and motorcycles with a capacity of 125cc or less are not allowed.
高速道路で渋滞などにより低速走行が続く場合は、最低速度規制が一時的に適用されなく: Minimum speed regulations will temporarily no longer apply if vehicles continue to drive at low speeds on expressways due to traffic jams.
交通状況により低速走行を強いられる場合は、最低速度を維持できないことが認められ、: If a vehicle is forced to drive at a low speed due to traffic conditions, it is recognized that the minimum speed cannot be maintained and it is not a violation of regulations.
高速道路の路側帯（路肩）は、緊急車両のためのスペースであり、原則として走行や駐停: The roadside strip (shoulder) of an expressway is a space for emergency vehicles, and as a general rule, they are not allowed to drive or park there.
路肩は故障車の退避や緊急車両通行のためのスペースであり、一般車両が走行や駐停車に: The road shoulder is a space for evacuation of broken down vehicles and for emergency vehicles to pass, and in principle, it is prohibited for general vehicles to use it for driving or parking.
高速道路を走行中に行き先を間違えた場合、その場で安全を確認してバックして元のイン: If you make a mistake while driving on the expressway, you can immediately check if it is safe and then back up and return to the original interchange.
高速道路上での後退や逆走は重大な事故を引き起こす危険な行為であり、絶対に行っては: Reversing or driving the wrong way on a highway is a dangerous act that can cause serious accidents, and you should never do it, and you should just continue to the next exit.
高速道路を走行する前には、燃料、冷却水、タイヤの空気圧などを点検しておくことが望: Before driving on the highway, it is advisable to check the fuel, coolant, tire pressure, etc.
高速道路は途中で停車することが難しいため、走行前に燃料・冷却水・タイヤ等の点検を: Since it is difficult to stop on the highway, you should check the fuel, coolant, tires, etc. before driving.
高速道路で前の車に追従する場合の車間距離は、速度が速くなるほど長くとる必要がある: When following a car in front on a highway, the faster the speed, the longer the distance between the cars needs to be.
速度が速くなるほど停止までに必要な距離が長くなるため、車間距離は速度に応じて十分: The faster the speed, the longer the distance required to stop, so the distance between vehicles needs to be wide enough depending on the speed.
高速道路から一般道路へ出る場合は、減速車線に入ってから速度を落とすのが正しい方法: When exiting a highway onto a local road, the correct method is to enter the deceleration lane and then slow down.
本線上で速度を落とすと後続車との追突の危険があるため、減速車線に入ってから徐々に: If you slow down on the main road, there is a risk of a rear-end collision with the vehicle behind you, so you should gradually reduce your speed after entering the deceleration lane.
高速道路の登坂車線は、速度の遅い大型車などのための車線であり、追い越しのために一: The uphill lane on an expressway is a lane for slow-moving large vehicles and is prohibited for general vehicles to pass.
登坂車線は坂道で速度が落ちる大型車等のための車線で、これを追い越し目的で使用する: The uphill lane is a lane for large vehicles that slow down on slopes, and is not intended to be used for overtaking purposes.
高速道路上で発生した火災の場合、消火が困難であれば車を路肩に止めてすぐに安全な場: In the case of a fire that occurs on a highway, if it is difficult to extinguish the fire, you should stop your car on the side of the road and evacuate to a safe location immediately.
高速道路上での火災時は無理に消火を試みず、ガードレールの外など安全な場所へ速やか: In the event of a fire on a highway, the priority is not to forcefully try to extinguish the fire, but rather to quickly evacuate to a safe location, such as outside a guardrail.
高速道路のサービスエリアやパーキングエリアでは、休憩のための駐車であれば長時間で: In expressway service areas and parking areas, there is no problem even if you park for a long time as long as you are just taking a break.
サービスエリア等は休憩や物資補給のための場所であり、長時間にわたる駐車は他の利用: Service areas are places for resting and replenishing supplies, and parking for long periods of time is a hindrance to other users and should be avoided.
高速道路の本線車道で故障し停止せざるを得ない場合、可能であれば路肩よりもさらに左: If you are forced to stop due to a breakdown on the main road of a highway, you should move to a safe location further to the left of the shoulder if possible.
本線上での停止は非常に危険なため、可能な限り路肩やさらに安全な退避スペースまで車: Stopping on the main road is extremely dangerous, so vehicles should be moved to the shoulder of the road or to a safer evacuation space if possible.
高速道路を走行中、眠気を感じたときはサービスエリアなどに入って休憩を取るべきであ: If you feel drowsy while driving on the expressway, you should go to a service area and take a break.
高速道路での居眠り運転は重大事故の原因となるため、眠気を感じたら速やかにサービス: Drowsy driving on the expressway can cause serious accidents, so if you feel drowsy, you should immediately take a rest at a service area.
高速道路でチェーン規制が出されている場合、スタッドレスタイヤを装着していればチェ: If there is a chain restriction on the expressway, you do not need to wear chains if you are wearing studless tires.
チェーン規制が発令されている区間では、スタッドレスタイヤだけでは通行が認められず: In areas where chain restrictions have been issued, drivers are not allowed to drive with only studded tires; tires must be equipped with chains.
高速道路での追い越しは、原則として右側の車線を使って行わなければならない。: As a general rule, overtaking on expressways must be done using the right lane.
日本の高速道路は左側通行のため、前車を追い越す際は右側の車線に出て追い越し、追い: Japanese expressways drive on the left, so when passing the vehicle in front of you, move into the right lane and quickly return to your original lane after passing.
高速道路の入口で「ETC専用」と表示されたゲートには、ETCを搭載していない車も: Vehicles not equipped with ETC can also enter the gates marked "ETC only' at expressway entrances.
ETC専用ゲートはETC搭載車のみが通行できるレーンであり、ETCを搭載していな: ETC-only gates are lanes where only ETC-equipped vehicles can pass, and if a vehicle without ETC enters, they will not be able to pass through, causing trouble.
高速道路の合流地点では、本線を走る車には合流車に対して進路を譲る法的義務がある。: At expressway junctions, vehicles traveling on the main road have a legal obligation to yield to merging vehicles.
合流地点では本線車に優先権があり、合流車側が本線の流れを見て安全な隙間に入る義務: At merging points, main line cars have priority, and the merging car has the obligation to follow the flow of the main line and move into a safe gap.
交差点で右折する際、対向車線に複数の車線がある場合でも、最も右側の車線にいる車だ: When turning right at an intersection, you only need to pay attention to the vehicle in the rightmost lane, even if there are multiple lanes of oncoming traffic.
対向車線が複数ある場合は、すべての車線から来る直進車やバイクなどに注意し、死角に: If there are multiple oncoming traffic lanes, it is necessary to pay attention to straight cars and motorcycles from all lanes, and to lanes that are prone to blind spots.
信号機のある交差点で青信号でも、すでに交差点内に渡り終えていない歩行者がいる場合: Even if there is a green light at an intersection with a traffic light, if there are pedestrians who have not yet crossed the intersection, they must not start.
信号が変わっても横断歩道や交差点内に渡り終えていない歩行者がいる場合は、その通行: If there are pedestrians who have not yet crossed the street at a crosswalk or intersection even after the traffic lights have changed, you must ensure that you do not obstruct their passage.
「動物が飛び出す」恐れのある区域を示す標識がある場合、運転者はそれに備えて速度を: If there is a sign indicating an area where animals may jump out, drivers should slow down and be prepared for it.
動物注意の標識がある区間では、野生動物が突然道路に飛び出す可能性があるため、速度: In areas with animal caution signs, you should slow down and be prepared as wild animals may suddenly jump onto the road.
信号のない交差点に同時に複数の方向から車が進入する場合、最も大きい車両が優先され: When vehicles enter an unsignalized intersection from multiple directions at the same time, the largest vehicle has priority.
車両の大きさは優先順位に関係せず、道幅・標識の有無・左方優先の原則などに基づいて: The size of the vehicle has no bearing on the priority order; the priority order is determined based on factors such as road width, the presence or absence of signs, and the principle of giving priority to the left side.
交差点で右折待ちの車は、対向車が途切れるまで交差点の中心の直近内側で待機してよい: Vehicles waiting to make a right turn at an intersection may wait just inside the center of the intersection until there is no oncoming traffic.
右折待ちの車両は交差点の中心の直近内側を通行することが原則として認められ、対向車: As a general rule, vehicles waiting to turn right are allowed to pass just inside the center of the intersection, waiting for oncoming traffic to pass before turning right.
交差点に「一時停止」の標識がない場合でも、見通しの悪い交差点では徐行して安全を確: Even if there is no "stop" sign at an intersection, you should drive slowly at intersections with poor visibility and check for safety.
標識の有無にかかわらず、見通しの悪い交差点では徐行して左右の安全を確認することが: Regardless of whether there are signs or not, the basics of safe driving are to drive slowly at intersections with poor visibility and check both sides for safety.
信号機のある交差点で黄色信号が点灯したとき、停止線の直前にいる車は安全に停止でき: When a yellow light turns on at an intersection with traffic lights, vehicles immediately in front of the stop line must stop if it is safe to do so.
黄色信号は原則停止を意味し、安全に停止できる場合は停止線の直前で停止しなければな: A yellow light generally means a stop, and if it is safe to do so, you must stop just before the stop line.
交差点内で進路を妨げられそうな場合、交差点が混雑していると分かっていても進入して: If your path is likely to be blocked at an intersection, you may enter the intersection even if you know it is busy.
交差点内で動けなくなるおそれがある場合は、信号が青であっても交差点に進入してはな: If there is a risk that you may become stuck within the intersection, do not enter the intersection even if the traffic light is green.
信号機が「赤の点滅」を表示している交差点では、車は一時停止して安全を確認してから: At intersections where the traffic lights are flashing red, cars can temporarily stop and ensure it is safe before proceeding.
赤の点滅信号は「止まれ」の標識と同じ意味であり、一時停止して安全確認後に進行でき: A flashing red signal has the same meaning as a "stop" sign, meaning you can stop temporarily and proceed after ensuring safety.
黄色の点滅信号がある交差点では、他の交通に注意して進行することができる。: At intersections with flashing yellow lights, you can proceed with caution towards other traffic.
黄色の点滅信号は、他の車両や歩行者の動きに注意しながら進行してよいことを示す。: A flashing yellow signal indicates that you may proceed while paying attention to the movements of other vehicles and pedestrians.
T字路で本線とぶつかる側道から進入する車は、本線を走る車に対して常に進路を譲らな: Vehicles approaching from a side road that meets the main line at a T-junction must always yield to vehicles traveling on the main line.
T字路では、突き当たる側の道路（側道）から進入する車は、本線（直進道路）を走る車: At a T-junction, vehicles approaching from the side road (service road) must yield to vehicles traveling on the main road (straight road).
交差点を右折する際は、あらかじめできるだけ道路の中央に寄って大回りで進入するのが: When making a right turn at an intersection, the correct way is to approach the center of the road as much as possible and take a wide turn.
右折時はあらかじめできるだけ道路中央に寄り、交差点の中心の直近内側を通る小回りで: When making a right turn, the correct way to do this is to move as close to the center of the road as possible and take a short turn around the center of the intersection.
交差点を左折する際は、できるだけ道路の左端に沿って小回りで進行しなければならない: When making a left turn at an intersection, you should make a short turn along the left edge of the road as much as possible.
左折時はできるだけ道路の左端に沿って小回りで進行することで、後続の二輪車などとの: When making a left turn, you can avoid accidents involving other motorcycles or other vehicles by following the left edge of the road as closely as possible.
前方の交差点で右折しようとしている対向車がいる場合、こちらが直進であれば速度や進: If there is an oncoming vehicle making a right turn at the intersection in front of you, you can pass without changing your speed or course as long as you are going straight.
直進車には優先権があるため、対向車が右折しようとしていても直進車は速度や進路を変: Vehicles traveling straight have the right of way, so even if an oncoming vehicle is about to turn right, a vehicle traveling straight must pass without changing speed or course, but always pay attention to the other vehicle's movements.
交差点付近で工事などにより車線が規制されている場合、規制のない車線を走る車が優先: When lanes are restricted near an intersection due to construction, etc., cars driving in unrestricted lanes are given priority.
車線規制がある場合は、状況に応じて係員の指示や標識に従い、規制側の車両にも適切に: If there are lane restrictions, priority should be given to safe passage, such as following the instructions of the staff and signs, and giving way to vehicles on the restricted side, depending on the situation.
矢印式の信号で右向きの矢印のみが点灯している場合、その時間帯は右折する車のみが進: If only the right arrow is lit on an arrow signal, only vehicles making a right turn may proceed during that time.
矢印信号は矢印の方向にのみ進行を許可するもので、右矢印のみが点灯している場合は右: Arrow signals only allow vehicles to proceed in the direction of the arrow, and if only the right arrow is lit, only right-turning vehicles can proceed.
信号機に「歩車分離式」と表示されている交差点では、車両用信号と歩行者用信号が同時: At intersections where the traffic lights indicate "pedestrian and vehicle separation", the vehicle and pedestrian signals do not turn green at the same time.
歩車分離式信号は車両と歩行者が同時に交差点を通行しないよう制御され、巻き込み事故: Pedestrian and vehicle separation signals prevent vehicles and pedestrians from passing through intersections at the same time, and prevent accidents involving pedestrians and pedestrians.
「左折可」の標示板がある交差点では、赤信号でも他の交通に注意して左折することがで: At intersections with "Left Turn Allowed" signs, you can make a left turn even if the light is red, paying attention to other traffic.
「左折可」の標示板がある場合は、赤信号でも他の車両や歩行者の通行を妨げないことを: If there is a sign that says "Left Turn Allowed,' you may turn left after making sure that you do not obstruct the passage of other vehicles or pedestrians even on red lights.
車両用信号が青で、その下にある「歩行者・自転車専用」の信号が赤の場合、自転車横断: If the vehicle traffic light is green and the "pedestrian/bicycle only" light below it is red, bicycles in the bicycle crossing zone must also stop.
歩行者・自転車専用の信号が赤であれば、自転車横断帯を通行する自転車もその信号に従: If the traffic light for pedestrians and bicycles is red, bicycles passing through the bicycle crossing zone must also stop according to the traffic light.
「中央線」を示す道路標示（黄色の実線）がある場所では、追い越しのためにその線を越: Where there is a road marking (solid yellow line) indicating the "center line,' do not cross the line to overtake.
黄色の実線は追い越しのための右側部分への進出を禁止する中央線であり、これを越えて: The solid yellow line is the center line that prohibits drivers from moving to the right side for overtaking, and overtaking beyond this line is prohibited.
白の破線の中央線は、対向車などの安全を確認できれば追い越しのために越えることが認: It is permitted to cross the dashed white center line to pass other vehicles if it is safe to do so.
白の破線で示された中央線がある場所では、安全が確認できれば追い越しのために線を越: Where there is a center line marked with a dashed white line, drivers may cross the line to overtake if it is safe to do so.
道路に書かれた菱形（ダイヤモンド）のマークは、前方に横断歩道や自転車横断帯がある: A diamond-shaped mark on the road warns you that there is a crosswalk or bicycle crossing zone ahead.
道路上の菱形マークは横断歩道・自転車横断帯ありの予告標示であり、運転者に注意を促: A diamond-shaped mark on the road is a warning sign that there is a crosswalk or bicycle crossing zone, and it alerts motorists.
重量制限は車両自体（車両総重量）に対する規制であり、空車かどうかにかかわらず表示: The weight limit is a restriction on the vehicle itself (total vehicle weight), and vehicles exceeding the indicated weight cannot pass, regardless of whether it is empty or not.
進行方向別通行区分の標示は車線ごとに進める方向を指定し、直進矢印のみの車線からは: Direction-based traffic classification signs specify the direction of travel for each lane, and it is not possible to turn right or left from a lane with only a straight arrow.
規制予告標識は、前方で始まる速度規制や追越し禁止などの内容を事前に知らせ、運転者: Restriction warning signs inform drivers of speed restrictions and overtaking restrictions that will begin ahead of them, encouraging drivers to prepare.
この警戒標識は学校や保育施設などが近くにあることを示し、子供の飛び出しに備えて速: These warning signs indicate nearby schools, child care facilities, etc. and remind drivers to slow down in case children run out.
「学校、幼稚園・保育所等あり」の警戒標識は、付近で子供の飛び出しに注意するよう運: The warning sign "School, Kindergarten, Daycare Center, etc.' reminds drivers to be careful of children running out in the vicinity.
「規制予告」の標示板は、その先で実際に規制が始まる地点とおおむね同じ規制内容を示: The "Regulation Notice" signboard indicates roughly the same regulations as the point at which the regulations actually begin.
「重量制限」の標識がある橋では、表示された重量を超える車両は、空車であれば通行し: On bridges with "weight limit" signs, vehicles that exceed the posted weight may be allowed to pass if they are empty.
進行方向別通行区分の標示がある車線で、直進矢印のみが描かれた車線からは右折しては: Do not turn right from a lane marked with a straight ahead arrow on it, where there are signs indicating traffic divisions based on direction of travel.
横断しようとしている歩行者がいる場合、ボタンの状態にかかわらず車は一時停止して歩: If a pedestrian is about to cross, the vehicle must not stop and obstruct the pedestrian's passage, regardless of the state of the button.
「停止禁止部分」の標示（黄色の格子状の枠）がある場所では、その内側で停止してはな: Where there is a "No Stopping Area" sign (yellow grid frame), do not stop inside it.
「自転車専用」の標識がある通行帯を普通自動車が走行することは認められている。: Regular cars are allowed to drive in lanes marked with "bicycles only' signs.
信号機のない押しボタン式の横断歩道では、ボタンが押されていなければ歩行者がいても: At push-button crosswalks without traffic lights, cars do not have to stop even if a pedestrian is present unless the button is pressed.
自転車専用の通行帯は自転車のための車線であり、普通自動車は原則として通行すること: Bicycle-only lanes are lanes for bicycles, and as a general rule, regular cars are not allowed to pass through them.
黄色の格子状の標示は交差点内での停滞を防ぐためのもので、前方の混雑が原因でその中: The yellow grid-shaped markings are intended to prevent vehicles from stopping at intersections, and it is prohibited to enter them and stop due to congestion in front of the intersection.
「専用通行帯」の標示がある路線バス専用車線は、原則としてバス以外のすべての車両が: As a general rule, all vehicles other than buses cannot pass through lanes designated for route buses that are marked as "exclusive lanes.'

黄色地に黒の三角形で示される警戒標識は、カーブや踏切など前方の危険を運転者に予告: Warning signs, which are black triangles on a yellow background, serve to warn drivers of dangers ahead, such as curves and railroad crossings.
警戒標識は、黄色地に黒枠の正三角形で表され、前方の危険な状況を予告するものである: A warning sign is an equilateral triangle with a black frame on a yellow background and warns of dangerous situations ahead.
「止まれ」の標識がある場所では、停止線の直前で完全に停止し、左右の安全を確認して: In areas where there is a "stop" sign, come to a complete stop right before the stop line, and check that it is safe on both sides before proceeding.
規制標識には、赤い縁取りの丸い標識だけでなく、青色の丸い標識もある。: Regulatory signs include not only round signs with a red border, but also round signs with a blue border.
「駐車禁止」の標識がある場所でも、5分以内の荷物の積み下ろしのために停車すること: Even if there is a "No Parking" sign, you are allowed to stop for up to five minutes to load or unload your luggage.
一方通行の標識は指定された一方向のみ通行を認めるもので、逆走は重大な違反となる。: One-way signs only allow traffic in one direction, and driving in the wrong direction is a serious violation.
「最低速度」や「専用通行帯」などの規制標識は青色の丸で示され、赤丸の禁止系標識と: Regulatory signs such as "minimum speed' and "exclusive lane' are indicated by blue circles, and have a different meaning from red circle prohibition signs.
逆三角形の「止まれ」の標識がある交差点では、停止線の直前で完全に停止しなければな: At intersections with inverted triangle "stop" signs, you must come to a complete stop just before the stop line.
「一方通行」の標識がある道路では、矢印で示された方向以外へ進行してはならない。: On roads marked with "one-way" signs, do not proceed in any direction other than the direction indicated by the arrow.
台風接近時に視界不良や強風で運転が危険と感じた場合、運転を中止して安全な場所に避: If you feel it is dangerous to drive due to poor visibility or strong winds when a typhoon approaches, it is appropriate to stop driving and evacuate to a safe location.
駐車禁止標識が規制するのは「駐車」であり、運転者がすぐ運転できる短時間の荷物の積: No-parking signs restrict parking, and do not prohibit short-term stops such as loading and unloading luggage that allow the driver to drive immediately.
悪天候下で危険を感じた場合は、無理に目的地へ向かわず、安全な場所に避難して状況の: If you feel unsafe under bad weather, the most appropriate decision is not to force your way to your destination, but rather to evacuate to a safe location and wait for the situation to improve.
「最低速度」の標識がある高速道路の区間では、表示された速度未満で走行してはならな: On sections of the highway that have "minimum speed" signs, do not drive below the posted speed.
補助標識は単独で設置されることはなく、必ず本標識と組み合わせて意味を補足するため: Auxiliary signs are never placed alone, but are always used in combination with the main sign to supplement the meaning.
「最高速度」の標識に表示された数字は、その区間で出してもよい速度の上限を示してい: The number displayed on the "Maximum Speed" sign indicates the upper limit of speed that can be reached in that section.
最高速度標識の数字は法定の上限速度を示し、これを超えて走行することは速度違反とな: The number on the maximum speed sign indicates the legal maximum speed, and driving in excess of this is a speeding violation.
「幅員減少」の標識は、警戒標識の一種で、前方の道路の幅が狭くなることを予告してい: A "width reduction" sign is a type of warning sign that warns you that the road ahead will become narrower.
「追い越し禁止」の標識がある区間では、前方の車両を追い越すために進路変更をして前: In areas where there is a "No Passing" sign, do not change course to pass the vehicle in front of you.
補助標識は「日曜・休日を除く」など、本標識に付随して規制の時間や区間などの詳細を: Auxiliary signs, such as "Excluding Sundays and Holidays,' serve to supplement details such as restricted hours and sections along with the main sign.
この標識は対向車線にはみ出す追い越しのみを禁止しており、同一車線内で追い越せる場: This sign only prohibits overtaking in the oncoming lane; it does not prohibit overtaking in the same lane.
「追い越しのための右側部分はみ出し通行禁止」の標識がある区間では、追い越し自体が: All overtaking is prohibited in areas where there is a sign that says "No passing on the right side for overtaking.'
幅員減少の標識がある場所では、道路が狭くなることを予測し、対向車との接触に注意し: In areas where there is a road width reduction sign, you should anticipate that the road will become narrower and reduce your speed to avoid collision with oncoming vehicles.
最低速度標識は交通の流れを維持するためのもので、表示された速度未満での走行は違反: Minimum speed signs are meant to keep traffic flowing; driving below the posted speed is a violation.
追い越し禁止の標識がある区間では、進路を変更して前車の前方に出る追い越し行為が禁: In areas where there are signs prohibiting overtaking, it is prohibited to change course and pass in front of the vehicle in front.
徐行とは、直ちに停止できるような速度で走行することを意味し、標識のある場所ではこ: Slowing down means driving at a speed that allows you to stop immediately, and you are obligated to slow down to this speed where there are signs.
「一時停止」の標識がある場所では、他に車も歩行者もいないと確認できても、必ず停止: You must stop wherever there is a "stop" sign, even if you can confirm that there are no other cars or pedestrians.
「徐行」の標識がある場所では、完全に停止する必要はないが、すぐに停止できる速度ま: Where there is a "slow down" sign, you do not have to come to a complete stop, but you must slow down to a speed that allows you to stop immediately.
この標識は大型貨物自動車など特定の車両のみを対象としており、普通乗用自動車は通行: This sign is only for specific vehicles such as large trucks, and regular passenger cars are allowed to pass.
「踏切あり」の標識は警戒標識であり、前方に鉄道の踏切があることを知らせている。: The "Railway Crossing" sign is a warning sign, informing you that there is a railroad crossing ahead.
一時停止標識がある場所では、周囲の状況にかかわらず必ず停止線の前で一時停止する義: Where there is a stop sign, you are obligated to stop in front of the stop line regardless of the surrounding conditions.
案内標識は、交通の規制や指示を目的とし、方向や地名などの情報は示さない。: Guide signs are for the purpose of regulating and directing traffic, and do not indicate information such as directions or place names.
Uターン禁止の標識は転回のみを禁止しており、通常の右折や左折までは禁止していない: A sign prohibiting U-turns only prohibits turning, not normal right or left turns.
案内標識は方向や地名、道路番号などの情報を提供するもので、規制や指示を目的とした: Guide signs provide information such as directions, place names, and road numbers, and are not intended to provide regulations or instructions.
踏切あり標識は前方に鉄道の踏切があることを予告し、運転者に速度を落として注意する: A railroad crossing sign warns drivers that there is a railroad crossing ahead and urges drivers to slow down and use caution.
「Uターン禁止」の標識がある区間では、転回はできないが、右折することは禁止されて: In sections with "No U-turns" signs, turning is not allowed, but right turns are not prohibited.
「大型貨物自動車等通行止め」の標識がある道路には、普通乗用自動車も通行することが: Regular passenger cars are also not allowed to drive on roads marked with "Large Trucks Closed" signs.
「路線バス等優先通行帯」の車線は、路線バス以外の車両は一切通行することができない: Vehicles other than route buses are not allowed to pass through the lanes designated as "priority lanes for route buses.'
「安全地帯」の標識がある場所は、路面電車の乗降客などの安全を確保するための区域で: Areas with "safety zone" signs are areas designed to ensure the safety of passengers getting on and off the streetcar.
規制標識と警戒標識では、警戒標識のほうが法的な拘束力が強く、必ず従わなければなら: Between regulatory signs and warning signs, warning signs have stronger legal binding force and must be obeyed.
「通行止め」の標識がある道路は、車両だけでなく歩行者もすべて通行することができな: Roads with "Closed" signs are closed to both vehicles and pedestrians.
「駐車余地」の標識に表示された数字は、駐車する際に道路の反対側に空けておくべき余: The number on the "Parking Room" sign indicates the width of the space in meters that should be left on the opposite side of the road when parking.
「優先道路」であることを示す標識がある道路は、交差する他の道路よりも優先的に通行: A road with a sign indicating that it is a "priority road" can be used preferentially over other intersecting roads.
警戒標識は危険を予告して注意を促すものであり、通行を直接禁止・指示する規制標識の: Warning signs warn of danger and call for caution, whereas regulatory signs that directly prohibit or instruct traffic have more legal binding force.
優先通行帯は他の車両も通行できるが、路線バス等が近づいたときは速やかに車線を譲ら: Other vehicles may pass through the priority lane, but you must yield to other vehicles as soon as possible when a bus or other vehicle approaches.
優先道路の標識がある道路を通行する車両は、交差点で一時停止や徐行の必要がなく、他: Vehicles traveling on roads with priority road signs do not need to stop or slow down at intersections and have priority over vehicles on other roads.
安全地帯は路面電車の乗降客や歩行者の安全を確保するために設けられた区域で、車両は: A safety zone is an area established to ensure the safety of tram passengers and pedestrians, and vehicles are not allowed to enter it.
「通行止め」は歩行者を含むすべての通行を禁止する規制であり、車両のみを対象とする: A "road closure" is a regulation that prohibits all traffic, including pedestrians, and is different from a "vehicle closure" that only targets vehicles.
この標識は、駐車した車と反対側の道路端との間に確保しなければならない余地の幅をメ: This sign indicates the width of the space in meters that must be left between a parked car and the opposite edge of the road.
優先道路を通行する車両は、交差する道路の車両に優先されるため、原則として交差点で: Vehicles traveling on priority roads have priority over vehicles on intersecting roads, so in principle there is no obligation to drive slowly at intersections.
山道での行き違いが困難な場合は、下り坂の車両が広い場所を利用して道を譲り、上り坂: If it is difficult to pass each other on a mountain road, the general rule is that the downhill vehicle uses a wide area to give way, and the uphill vehicle is allowed to pass first.
走行中の路面電車を追い越すことは原則禁止されており、乗客の乗降がある安全地帯付近: In principle, overtaking a running streetcar is prohibited, and special caution is required near safety zones where passengers are boarding and alighting.
明らかに道幅が狭い道路から広い道路に進入する場合は、広い道路を通行する車両が優先: When entering a wider road from an obviously narrow road, vehicles on the wider road have priority, so vehicles on the narrower side of the road must drive slowly to ensure safety.
路線バスが停留所から発進しようとして合図を出しているときは、後続車はできる限り進: When a route bus signals to depart from a bus stop, vehicles following it must yield as much of their path as possible.
サイレンを鳴らして走行する緊急自動車が近づいてきたときは、交差点を避けて道路の左: When an emergency vehicle approaches with its siren blaring, you must avoid the intersection, move to the left side of the road, and stop temporarily.
山道で行き違いが困難な場合、上り坂を走行している車両が道を譲り、下り坂の車両を先: If it is difficult to pass each other on a mountain road, the vehicle traveling uphill must give way and let the vehicle traveling downhill pass first.
緊急自動車が接近した場合、一般車両は交差点を避けて道路の左側端に寄り、緊急車両の: If an emergency vehicle approaches, regular vehicles should avoid the intersection, move to the left side of the road, and stop temporarily so as not to obstruct the passage of the emergency vehicle.
道路の幅が明らかに狭い道路から広い道路の交差点に進入する車両は、道路の幅にかかわ: A vehicle entering an intersection with a wider road from an obviously narrower road can pass through the intersection first, regardless of the width of the road.
路線バスが発進の合図を出しているときは、後続の車両はできる限りその進路変更に協力: When a route bus signals to start, vehicles following it must cooperate with the change of direction and yield the right of way as much as possible.
路面電車が走行しているときは、後方から進行する自動車は原則として追い越してはなら: As a general rule, when a streetcar is running, you must not overtake a car that is moving from behind.
優先道路であることを示す標識や道路標示がある道路を通行する車両は、交差点で徐行す: Vehicles traveling on roads with priority road signs or road markings do not need to slow down at intersections.
環状交差点に入る車両は徐行し、環道内をすでに通行している車両の進行を優先させなけ: Vehicles entering a roundabout must drive slowly and give priority to vehicles already traveling on the roundabout.
横断歩道を渡っている歩行者がいる場合、車両はその歩行者の通行を妨げてはならない。: If a pedestrian is crossing the road, vehicles must not obstruct the pedestrian's passage.
自転車横断帯を自転車が横断しているときでも、車両は自転車の通過を待たずに先に通行: Even when a bicycle is crossing a bicycle crossing zone, vehicles may pass through without waiting for the bicycle to pass.
横断歩道を渡っている、または渡ろうとしている歩行者がいる場合、車両は必ず一時停止: If a pedestrian is crossing or attempting to cross a crosswalk, vehicles must stop and give the pedestrian priority.
右折する車両は対向直進車・左折車に加えて、右折先の横断歩道を渡る歩行者や自転車の: Vehicles making a right turn must not obstruct the flow of oncoming vehicles going straight on or turning left, as well as pedestrians and bicycles crossing the crosswalk where they are turning right.
ラウンドアバウト（環状交差点）に進入する車両は、すでに環道を通行している車両の進: Vehicles entering a roundabout must not obstruct the movement of vehicles already on the roundabout.
日本の道路交通法には、アメリカのようにスクールバス停車時に後続車が必ず一時停止す: Although Japan's road traffic law does not require vehicles following a school bus to stop temporarily like it does in the United States, it is important to be aware of the safety of those around you.
私有地や駐車場から道路に出る車両は、道路標示の有無にかかわらず、道路を走行する車: Vehicles exiting onto the road from private property or parking lots must not impede the flow of vehicles on the road, regardless of road markings.
右折待ちの車両は、対向車線の直進車や左折車だけでなく、横断中の歩行者や自転車にも: Vehicles waiting to turn right must yield not only to oncoming vehicles traveling straight or turning left, but also to pedestrians and bicycles crossing the road.
私有地の駐車場から道路に出るときは、道路標示に優先道路の表示がなければ、道路を走: When exiting a private parking lot onto the road, you may exit ahead of other vehicles on the road unless there is a road sign indicating that you have the right of way.
自転車横断帯を自転車が横断しているときは、車両はその通過を妨げないよう一時停止し: When a bicycle is crossing a bicycle crossing zone, vehicles must stop and wait so as not to obstruct the bicycle's passage.
スクールバスが停車して子供を乗り降りさせているとき、後続車は日本の法律上、必ず一: When a school bus stops to allow children to get on or off, vehicles following it must stop temporarily under Japanese law.
左折する車両は、その左折先の道路を直進する自転車や横断する歩行者の通行を優先させ: Vehicles making a left turn must give priority to bicycles proceeding straight on the road they are turning to and pedestrians crossing the road.
消防用防火水槽や消火栓から5メートル以内は駐車が禁止されており、この基準の中に止: It is prohibited to park within 5 meters of a fire prevention water tank or fire hydrant, and parking within this standard is a violation.
原動機付自転車の法定最高速度は、標識による特別な指定がない限り時速30キロメート: The legal maximum speed for mopeds is 30 km/h unless otherwise specified by signs.
左折時は、巻き込み事故を防ぐため、左折先の直進する自転車や横断歩道を渡る歩行者を: When making a left turn, you must give priority to cyclists going straight ahead at the left turn and pedestrians crossing the crosswalk to prevent accidents.
交差点の側端から5メートル以内は駐停車禁止区域であり、緊急車両の通行や見通しの確: Parking is prohibited within 5 meters from the side edge of an intersection, and this area is established to ensure the passage of emergency vehicles and visibility.
一時停止の標識がなく道幅もほぼ同じ交差点で、両方の車両が同時に交差点に到達した場: If both vehicles arrive at the intersection at the same time and there is no stop sign and the roads are approximately the same width, the larger vehicle will have priority.
道幅がほぼ同じ交差点で両車が同時に到達した場合、車両の大きさに関係なく、左方から: If both vehicles arrive at an intersection with approximately the same width at the same time, priority will be given to the vehicle coming from the left, regardless of vehicle size.
ひし形の白線は優先道路であることを示す標示で、これがある道路を通行する車両は他の: A diamond-shaped white line indicates a priority road, and vehicles using this road have priority over vehicles on other intersecting roads.
交差点内に優先道路を示す道路標示（ひし形の白線）がある場合、その道路を通行する車: If there is a road marking (diamond-shaped white line) indicating a priority road within an intersection, vehicles traveling on that road will have priority.
交差点の側端から5メートル以内の道路には、駐車も停車もしてはならない。: Vehicles may not park or stop on the road within 5 meters of the side edge of an intersection.
消火栓や消防用防火水槽の位置から5メートル以内であれば、駐車しても違反にならない: It is not a violation to park within 5 meters of a fire hydrant or fire prevention water tank.
標識による指定がない一般道路での原動機付自転車の最高速度は、時速30キロメートル: The maximum speed for mopeds on public roads that are not designated by signs is 30 kilometers per hour.
坂の頂上付近や勾配の急な坂道は、見通しが悪く危険なため、追い越しの有無にかかわら: Because visibility is poor and dangerous near the top of a hill or on a steep slope, parking or stopping is prohibited, regardless of whether or not you are passing.
高速道路の本線車道以外の一般道路において、標識で特に指定がない場合の法定最高速度: On general roads other than the main lanes of expressways, the legal maximum speed is 60 kilometers per hour unless otherwise specified by a sign.
標識等で特に指定がない一般道路では、普通自動車の法定最高速度は時速60キロメート: On general roads that are not specifically designated by signs, the legal maximum speed for ordinary vehicles is set at 60 kilometers per hour.
駐車する際は、他の交通の妨げにならないように道路の左端に沿って止め、道路と並行に: When parking, it is necessary to park along the left edge of the road and parallel to the road so as not to obstruct other traffic.
トンネルの中は、車両の交換所として指定された場所を除き、駐車も停車も禁止されてい: No parking or parking is permitted inside the tunnel, except in designated areas for vehicle exchange.
横断歩道付近での駐停車は歩行者の視界を妨げるため、前後5メートル以内は駐停車禁止: Parking or parking near a crosswalk obstructs pedestrians' visibility, so parking within 5 meters in front or in front of the pedestrian crossing is prohibited.
横断歩道またはその端から前後5メートル以内の場所は、駐停車が禁止されている。: Parking is prohibited within 5 meters of a crosswalk or its edge.
バス停留所の標識がある位置から10メートル以内であれば、バスの運行時間外はいつで: You can always park within 10 meters of a bus stop sign outside of bus service hours.
坂の頂上付近や勾配の急な坂道は、追い越しをしなければ駐車しても問題ない。: There is no problem parking near the top of a hill or on a steep slope as long as you do not overtake.
トンネル内は視界が悪く事故につながりやすいため、指定された場所を除いて駐停車が禁: Because visibility is poor inside tunnels and can easily lead to accidents, parking is prohibited except in designated areas.
駐車の際は道路の左端に沿って車両を止め、他の交通を妨げないようにしなければならな: When parking, vehicles must be parked along the left edge of the road so as not to obstruct other traffic.
バス停留所の標識から10メートル以内は、運行時間に関係なく常に駐停車が禁止されて: Parking or stopping within 10 meters of a bus stop sign is prohibited at all times, regardless of operating hours.
坂道での駐車では、パーキングブレーキだけでなく、タイヤに輪止めをかけるなどの追加: When parking on a slope, it is advisable to use not only the parking brake but also additional measures to prevent rolling, such as chocking the tires.
駐車余地の標示がある場所では、指定された余地を必ず確保しなければ、たとえ短時間で: In areas where parking spaces are marked, you will not be allowed to park, even for a short time, unless you secure the designated space.
「駐車余地」の標示がある場所では、対象の余地を確保しなくても、運転者が短時間で戻: In areas where there is a "parking space" sign, the driver may park his or her car without having to secure a parking space as long as the driver returns within a short time.
駐車するときは、サイドブレーキ（駐車ブレーキ）を確実にかけ、車両が動き出さないよ: When parking, make sure to apply the handbrake (parking brake) to prevent the vehicle from moving.
坂道に駐車する際は、車両が動き出さないよう、タイヤに輪止めをかけるなどの措置をと: When parking on a slope, it is recommended to take measures such as chocking the tires to prevent the vehicle from moving.
標識のない住宅街の道路であっても、他の交通の妨げになる場所には駐車してはならない: Do not park where it obstructs other traffic, even on unmarked residential roads.
高速道路の本線車道での駐停車は、故障や事故などやむを得ない場合を除き、原則として: As a general rule, parking on the main road of an expressway is prohibited, except in unavoidable cases such as breakdowns or accidents.
高速道路の本線車道では、故障などのやむを得ない場合を除き、路肩に駐停車することは: On expressway main lanes, parking on the shoulder of the road is permitted, except in unavoidable circumstances such as a breakdown.
火災報知機がある位置から1メートル以内の場所は、駐車が禁止されている。: Parking is prohibited within 1 meter of a fire alarm.
標識の有無にかかわらず、他の交通の妨げになるような場所への駐車は法律で禁止されて: It is prohibited by law to park in any area that obstructs other traffic, regardless of whether there is a sign or not.
火災報知機の使用を妨げないよう、その位置から1メートル以内は駐車禁止区域とされて: To avoid interfering with the use of fire alarms, parking is prohibited within 1 meter of the fire alarm's location.

押しボタン式信号は歩行者がボタンを押すことで信号が変わる仕組みであり、押さなけれ: A push-button signal is a system in which a pedestrian presses a button to change the signal, and it may not turn green unless the pedestrian presses the button.

二輪車を含む車両は、右折する際あらかじめ道路の中央（一方通行では右端）に寄り、交: When making a right turn, vehicles, including motorcycles, must first approach the center of the road (the far right on one-way streets), pass just inside the center of the intersection, and make a short right turn.
追越し禁止の標識は自動車同士の追い越しを規制するものであり、軽車両（自転車）を追: The no-passing sign restricts passing between cars; passing light vehicles (bicycles) itself is not prohibited. However, it is necessary to ensure safe lateral distance.
信号機のない踏切に接近したときは、二輪車であっても必ず踏切の手前で一時停止し、安: When approaching a railroad crossing without traffic lights, even if you are riding a motorcycle, you must stop before the crossing and check for safety.
右折の手信号は、右腕を水平に伸ばす方法と、左腕を出して肘を垂直に上げる方法のいず: The hand signal for a right turn can be expressed either by extending the right arm horizontally or by extending the left arm and raising the elbow vertically.
踏切では信号機の有無にかかわらず、すべての車両は踏切の直前で一時停止し、左右の安: At a railroad crossing, regardless of whether there is a traffic light or not, all vehicles must stop immediately before the crossing and check that both sides are safe before crossing.
右折を表す手による合図は、右腕を水平に伸ばす、または左腕を車外に出して肘を垂直に: A hand signal indicating a right turn can be made by extending the right arm horizontally, or by extending the left arm out of the vehicle and raising the elbow vertically.
停車中の路線バスが発進の合図をしたときは、後方車両は速度を落とすなどしてその発進: When a parked route bus signals to start, vehicles behind must slow down so as not to interfere with the start of the bus.
二段階右折が指定された交差点では、原動機付自転車はまず直進して道路の左端に出て向: At intersections where two-stage right turns are designated, mopeds must first go straight, exit to the left edge of the road, turn around, and then turn right according to the oncoming traffic lights; short right turns are prohibited.
カーブの手前では、まずアクセルを戻し、必要に応じて前後ブレーキを併用して速度を落: Before entering a curve, it is best to first release the accelerator and use both the front and rear brakes as necessary to slow down.
二輪車が他の車を追い越すときは、追い越される車の右側を通行しなければならない。: When a motorcycle passes another vehicle, it must pass on the right side of the vehicle being passed.
停止する直前は、ハンドルを切ったまま停止すると足をつきやすく安定する。: Just before stopping, keep the steering wheel turned to make it easier to find your footing and stabilize the vehicle.
けん引中の自動車は、二輪車にとって追い越しの優先対象とはならないため、自由に追い: Motorcycles that are being towed do not have priority overtaking for motorcycles, so they may freely pass them.
停車中の路線バスが発進の合図をしているとき、後方の二輪車はバスの発進を妨げないよ: When a parked route bus is giving the signal to start, motorcycles behind the bus must not interfere with the bus's departure.
下り坂のカーブでは、エンジンブレーキを使わずフロントブレーキのみで速度調整するの: On downhill curves, it is safest to adjust the speed using only the front brake without using engine braking.
けん引中の自動車を追い越す場合も、通常の追い越しと同様に前方や対向車の安全を十分: When passing a car that is being towed, you must check the safety of the vehicle ahead and oncoming, just as you would for normal overtaking, and you are not free to overtake without conditions.
カーブの手前ではアクセルを戻して減速を始め、前後ブレーキを適切に併用して安全な速: The basic rule is to start decelerating by releasing the accelerator before entering the curve, and use both the front and rear brakes appropriately to reduce the speed to a safe level before entering the curve.
追い越しは原則として前車の右側から行わなければならず、左側からの追い越し（追い抜: As a general rule, overtaking must be done from the right side of the vehicle in front, and overtaking (overtaking) from the left side is generally prohibited.
停止する直前はハンドルを正面に向けてから停止する方が車体が安定し、ハンドルが切れ: It is better to turn the steering wheel toward the front before stopping, as this will make the vehicle more stable; if the steering wheel is left turned, it is easier to lose balance unexpectedly.
下り坂のカーブでは、エンジンブレーキと前後ブレーキを適切に併用して速度を一定に保: On downhill curves, it is advisable to use a combination of engine braking and front and rear brakes to maintain a constant speed; relying solely on the front brake increases the risk of fading or falling.
高速道路のカーブは大きな半径で設計されていることが多く、速度も高いため、無理に傾: Expressway curves are often designed with large radii and speeds are high, so it is important to drive at a speed and route that allows plenty of leeway without making the slope angle too large.
S字状の道路やクランク（屈折）コースを通行するときは、ハンドルではなく主に体重移: When driving on S-shaped roads or crank courses, the vehicle is stabilized primarily by shifting its weight and adjusting the accelerator, rather than by using the steering wheel.
S字やクランクのような低速での切り返しでは、ハンドル操作だけに頼らず、目線・体重: When making low-speed turns such as S-curves or crank turns, it is important to maintain stability by not only relying on steering wheel operation, but also through a combination of line of sight, weight shift, constant accelerator and clutch operation.
二人乗りのときは車体の重心が高くなり、ブレーキの効きも変わるため、一人乗りのとき: When riding with two people, the center of gravity of the vehicle is higher and the effectiveness of the brakes changes, so it is necessary to drive at a slower speed than when riding alone and negotiate curves with more leeway.
連続するカーブ（つづら折り）では、一つ目のカーブを抜けたらすぐに次のカーブの形状: In a series of curves (a zigzag), as soon as you exit the first curve, you may accelerate to the maximum regardless of the shape of the next curve.
発進時は半クラッチの状態を保ちながらアクセルを少しずつ開けることで、エンジンの力: When starting, by keeping the clutch in a partially engaged state and gradually opening the accelerator, the power of the engine is transmitted smoothly and the vehicle can be stabilized before starting.
二人乗りのときは、一人乗りのときと同じ感覚でカーブを通行してよい。: When riding with two people, you can negotiate curves in the same way as when riding alone.
停止時は両足を地面につけて車体を支え、バランスを保つことが基本的な操作である。: When stopping, the basic operation is to keep both feet on the ground to support the vehicle and maintain balance.
高速道路のカーブでは、一般道のカーブよりも傾斜角を大きくして通行するのが基本であ: In curves on expressways, it is basic to drive at a larger angle of inclination than on curves on general roads.
発進するときは、半クラッチを使いながらゆっくりアクセルを開けると安定しやすい。: When starting, it is easier to stabilize the vehicle by slowly opening the accelerator while using the clutch halfway.
停止しているときは、両足を地面につけてバランスをとるのが基本である。: When stopped, the basic idea is to keep both feet on the ground for balance.
夜間に対向車のライトで自分が見えにくくなることはあるが、二輪車のライトの位置自体: Although it may be difficult to see yourself at night due to the lights of an oncoming vehicle, the position of your motorcycle's lights itself will not cause you to be overlooked by an oncoming vehicle.
連続するカーブでは、次のカーブの形状や見通しを常に確認しながら速度を調整する必要: When navigating a series of curves, it is necessary to adjust speed while constantly checking the shape and outlook of the next curve, and it is dangerous to accelerate to maximum speed immediately after passing the first curve.
「警笛鳴らせ」の標識がある区間では、その場所を通行するすべての車両が必ず警音器を: In areas where there is a "Sound your horn" sign, all vehicles passing through that area must sound their horns.
他の車のすぐ後ろに連続して接近して走行すると、その車の運転者から二輪車が見えにく: If you drive closely behind another vehicle, it may be difficult for the driver of that vehicle to see your motorcycle.
大型車両は車体が大きいため、後方や左側面など運転席から直接見えない死角が多く、二: Because large vehicles have large bodies, there are many blind spots such as the rear and left side that cannot be seen directly from the driver's seat, and motorcycles must be careful not to enter these blind spots.
二輪車は車体が小さいため、自動車の運転者から見落とされやすく、自らライトを点灯し: Because motorcycles are small, they are easily overlooked by motorists, so it is important that they turn on their lights to alert them to their presence.
前車のすぐ後ろに付くと、その運転者のミラーの死角に入りやすく、二輪車の存在が見え: If you follow directly behind the vehicle in front of you, you are likely to be in the blind spot of the driver's mirror, and there is a risk that you will not be able to see the motorcycle, so it is important to maintain a safe following distance and take a position where you can see the vehicle.
二輪車のヘッドライトは1つで車幅が狭いため、対向車の運転者から遠くの一点光源と誤: Motorcycles have only one headlight and are narrow, so drivers of oncoming vehicles can easily misinterpret them as a distant light source, which can lead to misjudged distance and speed.
夜間に対向車のライトで自分が見えにくくなることはあるが、二輪車のライトの位置自体: Although it may be difficult to see yourself at night due to the lights of an oncoming vehicle, the position of your motorcycle's lights itself will not cause you to be overlooked by an oncoming vehicle.
二輪車は自動車に比べて車体が小さく速度判断もされにくいため、ライトの点灯や明るい: Motorcycles are smaller than cars, making it difficult to judge their speed, so alerting other drivers to your presence by turning on your lights and wearing bright clothing can help prevent accidents.
「警笛鳴らせ」の標識がある場所では、見通しの悪いカーブや踏切でなくても常にクラク: You must always honk your horn in areas where there is a "honk your horn' sign, even if you are not at a curve with poor visibility or at a railroad crossing.
大型トラックの運転席の真後ろや左側面は、ドライバーから見えにくい死角になりやすい: The area directly behind the driver's seat and on the left side of a large truck tends to become a blind spot where it is difficult for the driver to see.
停車中のバスやトラックの脇から出てくる歩行者は、ドライバーだけでなく二輪車にとっ: Pedestrians emerging from the side of parked buses and trucks can be difficult to see, not only for drivers but also for motorcyclists.
二輪車は車体が小さく速度感覚をつかみにくいため、自動車の運転者は二輪車との距離を: Because motorcycles have small bodies and it is difficult to get a sense of their speed, motor vehicle drivers tend to feel that the distance from the motorcycle is farther than it actually is.
ミラーには映らない死角が必ず存在するため、進路変更や右左折の際はミラー確認に加え: There are always blind spots that are not reflected in the mirror, so when changing course or turning left or right, in addition to checking the mirror, it is necessary to shake your head and visually check.
対向右折車は奥から接近する二輪車を見落として右折を始めることがあるため、二輪車側: Oncoming right-turning vehicles may overlook motorcycles approaching from the rear and start turning, so motorcycles must drive defensively by slowing down and maintaining eye contact.
交差点で右折待ちをしている対向車がいる場合、二輪車は車体が小さいため、その運転者: If there is an oncoming vehicle waiting to turn right at an intersection, there is a risk that the motorcycle driver will miss you and make a right turn because the motorcycle is small.
視界が悪くなる雨天時や夕暮れ時には、明るい色や反射材付きの装備を身につけることで: During rainy weather or at dusk, when visibility is poor, wearing brightly colored or reflective gear will make you more visible to other drivers and help prevent accidents.
雨の日や夕暮れ時は、二輪車の視認性が下がるため、より明るい色のヘルメットやジャケ: On rainy days and at dusk, visibility of motorcycles is reduced, so it is recommended to wear a lighter-colored helmet or jacket.
二輪車のライダーは、自分のミラーで後方を確認したらそれだけで十分であり、首を振っ: Motorcycle riders only need to check what's behind them in their mirrors; they don't need to turn their heads to check.
車体の小さい二輪車は実際よりも遠くにあるように見えやすく、自動車の運転者がその距: Small-sized motorcycles tend to appear farther away than they actually are, and motorists misjudge the distance and enter intersections, contributing to accidents.
二輪車のライダーは、運転中に体を動かしてミラーで見えない範囲を目視で確認する習慣: Motorcycle riders should get into the habit of moving their bodies while driving to visually check areas that cannot be seen in the mirror.
ミラーには映らない死角があるため、進路変更や右左折の前には首を動かして目視で確認: Since there are blind spots that are not reflected in mirrors, habits such as the "shelter check,' in which you move your head to visually check before changing course or turning left or right, will lead to safety.
停車している大型車両は歩行者の姿を隠してしまうため、二輪車もその脇を通過する際は: Large parked vehicles hide pedestrians, so motorcycles must slow down when passing by them and be prepared for pedestrians to jump out.
トンネルの出入口付近は、明暗の差が大きく、一時的に視界が悪くなることがある。: There is a large difference in light and darkness near the entrance and exit of the tunnel, and visibility may be temporarily reduced.
マンホールのふたや白線（ペイント）の上は、雨天時は特に滑りやすくなることがある。: Manhole covers and painted white lines can become slippery, especially in the rain.
鉄道の線路（路面電車の軌道）を斜めに横切ると、タイヤが溝にはまりやすく転倒の危険: If you cross railroad tracks (streetcar tracks) diagonally, your tires are likely to get stuck in the grooves, putting you at risk of falling.
工事現場などにある鉄板（仮設の覆い）の上を通過するときは、できるだけ速度を上げて: When passing over a steel plate (temporary cover) at a construction site, etc., it is safer to increase your speed as much as possible and pass over it all at once.
工事現場の鉄板は滑りやすく段差もあるため、速度を落とし、車体を垂直に保ったまま直: The steel plates at the construction site are slippery and there are some steps, so it is safe to slow down and slowly pass in a straight line while keeping your vehicle vertical.
車線中央には他車のオイルや砂利が溜まりやすいことがあるため、路面状況や周囲の安全: Oil and gravel from other vehicles can easily accumulate in the center of the lane, so it is advisable to gently adjust your driving position while keeping an eye on road conditions and the safety of your surroundings.
車線の中央を走行すると、路面の凹凸や砂利の影響を受けやすいことがあるため、状況に: If you drive in the center of the lane, you may be susceptible to uneven road surfaces or gravel, so it is best to adjust your driving position depending on the situation.
トンネルの出入口では明るさが急に変わるため、目が慣れるまで一時的に見えにくくなり: At tunnel entrances and exits, the brightness changes suddenly, making it temporarily difficult to see until your eyes adjust, so you need to slow down and be prepared.
路肩には砂利や落ち葉、ゴミなどが溜まりやすく、グリップが低下するため、二輪車は左: Gravel, fallen leaves, garbage, etc. tend to accumulate on the road shoulder, reducing grip, so motorcycles need to adjust their riding position within a safe range rather than riding on the edge of the left edge.
路面電車の軌道を斜めに横切ると前輪が溝にはまり、ハンドルを取られて転倒する危険が: If you cross the tram tracks diagonally, your front wheels may get stuck in the groove, causing you to lose your grip and fall, so you should cross the tram tracks at an angle as close to a right angle as possible.
金属製のマンホールのふたや塗装された白線は、雨で濡れるとアスファルトよりも摩擦が: When metal manhole covers and painted white lines get wet from rain, they have less friction than asphalt, making tires slippery.
車道の左端を走行する際、路肩の砂利や落ち葉が溜まっている部分は中央寄りの走行位置: When driving on the left edge of the road, the part of the road where gravel and fallen leaves have accumulated is often more slippery than when driving near the center.
対向車から自分の存在をよく見てもらうためには、車線の中で他車に隠れない位置を選ん: In order to make your presence more visible to oncoming vehicles, it is best to choose a position within the lane where you are not hidden by other vehicles.
わだち（車輪のくぼみ）ができている道路では、わだちの中を走るよりも、わだちの間（: On roads with ruts (wheel divots), it is always safer to drive between the ruts (raised areas) than through the ruts.
わだちの状態は路面や天候によって異なり、雨天時にはわだちに水がたまり、盛り上がっ: The condition of the ruts varies depending on the road surface and weather, and during rainy weather, water can collect in the ruts and the raised areas can become slippery, so it is not always safe to stay in the same position, so it is necessary to judge the situation.
落ち葉や砂が積もった路面は、晴天時でもタイヤと路面の間に薄い層を作りグリップを低: Roads covered with fallen leaves and sand create a thin layer between the tires and the road, reducing grip, even on sunny days, so drivers need to drive at a slower speed.
マンホールや白線、ペイント部分は雨の日は特に滑りやすくなるため、二輪車はできるだ: Manholes, white lines, and painted areas can become especially slippery on rainy days, so motorcycles should avoid passing them as much as possible.
降り始めの雨は路面の油膜を浮き上がらせ、四輪車より接地面積が小さい二輪車は転倒の: When it starts to rain, the oil film on the road surface is lifted, and two-wheeled vehicles, which have a smaller ground contact area than four-wheeled vehicles, are especially at risk of falling.
雨が降り始めた直後は、路面の油分とほこりが浮き上がって非常に滑りやすくなるため、: Motorcycles must be especially careful right after it starts raining, as oil and dust on the road surface becomes very slippery.
高速道路の合流地点では、本線を走る車から見えやすい位置を維持しながら速度を合わせ: At expressway merging points, it is best to merge at the same speed while maintaining a position that is easily visible to cars traveling on the main road.
合流時は本線車両の死角に入らず、見える位置で速度を合わせてから合流することで、追: When merging, you can reduce the risk of rear-end collisions or collisions by adjusting your speed at a spot where you can see the main line vehicles, and not in their blind spots.
前車に隠れる位置を走行すると対向車や交差車両から発見されにくくなるため、視界が確: If you drive behind the vehicle in front of you, you will be less likely to be spotted by oncoming or crossing vehicles, so being aware of the lane position that allows you to have a clear view will lead to safe driving.
道路の中央付近に積もった落ち葉や砂は、晴れた日でも乾いた路面より滑りやすい場合が: Fallen leaves and sand that accumulate near the center of the road can be more slippery than dry roads even on sunny days.
雨の日、マンホールや白線の上はタイヤが滑りやすいため、できるだけ避けて通行するの: On rainy days, tires can be slippery over manholes and white lines, so it's best to avoid them as much as possible.
濃い霧の中では他の車両から見えにくいため、ライトを点灯して自車の存在を知らせるこ: In dense fog, it is difficult for other vehicles to see you, so it is important for your safety to turn on your lights to alert you to your vehicle.
雨天時はタイヤのグリップが低下しているため、前後輪のブレーキを優しく、特に前輪は: In rainy weather, tire grip is reduced, so it is necessary to apply the brakes on the front and rear wheels gently, especially on the front wheels, to prevent slipping.
落ち葉が積もった道路は、晴れた日でも湿った落ち葉の下が滑りやすいため、二輪車は注: Roads covered with fallen leaves can be slippery even on sunny days, so motorcyclists need to be careful when riding on them.
二輪車は風の影響を受けやすく、強い横風では転倒の危険があるため、速度を落として風: Motorcycles are susceptible to the effects of wind, and strong crosswinds can cause them to fall over, so it is necessary to reduce speed and prepare for the wind.
強い横風が吹く橋の上では、二輪車はハンドルを取られやすいため速度を落とし、車体を: On bridges where strong crosswinds blow, it is easy for motorcycles to lose control of the steering wheel, so it is best to slow down and lean the vehicle in preparation.
落ち葉の下は湿気がこもりやすく非常に滑りやすいため、見た目では分からない危険とし: Moisture tends to accumulate under fallen leaves and it can be very slippery, so motorcyclists need to be especially careful as this is an invisible danger.
夏の猛暑日にアスファルトが軟化している場合、二輪車のタイヤが沈み込みハンドルが取: If the asphalt is softened on a hot summer day, the tires of a motorcycle may sink, making it easier to lose the steering wheel.
雪道や凍結した道路では、二輪車での走行は避けるべきであり、可能であれば車両の利用: Riding a motorcycle on snowy or icy roads should be avoided, and if possible, it is desirable to refrain from using a vehicle.
猛暑でアスファルトが軟化すると路面が不安定になり、特に二輪車は影響を受けやすいた: As the asphalt softens due to intense heat, the road surface becomes unstable, and motorcycles are especially susceptible to the effects, so they need to drive at reduced speeds.
濃い霧の中を走行する際は、テールランプやフォグランプを点灯して自車の存在を周囲に: When driving in thick fog, it's a good idea to turn on your tail lights and fog lights to alert others to your vehicle's presence.
二輪車は接地面積が小さく雪道や凍結路面では非常に転倒しやすいため、走行を避けるこ: It is recommended to avoid riding motorcycles on snowy or icy roads, as they have a small ground contact area and are extremely prone to tipping over.
雨天時、二輪車はブレーキをかける際に前後輪に均等に強い力をかけるとよい。: In rainy weather, when applying the brakes on a two-wheeled vehicle, it is a good idea to apply strong force evenly to the front and rear wheels.
道路交通法により、二輪車の運転者および同乗者はヘルメットの着用が義務付けられてい: According to the Road Traffic Act, motorcycle drivers and passengers are required to wear helmets.
暑くても転倒時の皮膚保護のため、長袖・長ズボンなど肌を覆う服装を着用することが安: Even in hot weather, it is recommended for safety to wear clothing that covers the skin, such as long sleeves and long pants, to protect the skin in the event of a fall.
夏の暑い日は、長袖・長ズボンを着用すると暑いため、半袖や短いズボンで運転する方が: On hot summer days, wearing long sleeves and long pants can get hot, so it is safer to drive in short sleeves and short pants.
あごひもを正しく締めていないヘルメットは事故時に脱落する危険があり、正しい着用と: A helmet whose chinstrap is not properly tightened is at risk of falling off in the event of an accident, and is not considered to be worn correctly.
ヘルメットはあごひもを正しく締めずに被るだけでも法律上の着用義務を満たしたことに: Just wearing a helmet without properly tightening the chinstrap means that the legal obligation to wear one is met.
二輪車を運転する際は、運転者・同乗者ともにヘルメットの着用が法律で義務付けられて: When driving a motorcycle, both the driver and passenger are required by law to wear a helmet.
