「並進可」の補助標識がある道路では、原動機付自転車が二台並んで走行することが認め: Two mopeds are allowed to ride side by side on roads with supplementary signs that say "side by side is allowed."
見通しの悪い交差点で「一時停止」の標識がある場合、二輪車も必ず停止線で停止しなけ: If there is a stop sign at an intersection with poor visibility, motorcycles must also stop at the stop line.
「専用通行帯（二輪車・原付を除く）」の標示がある車線には、原動機付自転車は通行す: mopeds are allowed to pass in lanes marked as "Exclusive lanes (excluding motorcycles and mopeds).'
「自動二輪車・原付以外の自動車通行止め」の標識がある道路は、自動二輪車と原付だけ: Only motorcycles and mopeds can pass on roads with signs that say "Closed to vehicles other than motorcycles and mopeds.'
この標識は二輪車以外の自動車の通行を禁止するもので、自動二輪車と原動機付自転車の: This sign prohibits all vehicles other than motorcycles from passing, and only motorcycles and mopeds are allowed to pass.
「二輪車・原付を除く」と表示された専用通行帯には、二輪車と原動機付自転車は通行す: Motorcycles and mopeds are not allowed to pass through designated lanes marked "Excluding motorcycles and mopeds.'
信号のある交差点で「二段階右折」の標示がある場合、原付は右折方向の信号が青でも直: If there is a "two-step right turn' sign at an intersection with a traffic light, the moped should move into the straight lane and wait, even if the right turn signal is green.
二段階右折の場所では、原付はまず交差点の対岸まで直進してから停止し、次の信号で進: At a two-stage right turn, the moped first goes straight to the opposite bank of the intersection, then stops, then changes direction at the next traffic light to complete the right turn.
一時停止の標識は二輪車にも適用され、停止線の直前で完全に停止する義務がある。: Stop signs also apply to motorcycles, which require them to come to a complete stop just before the stop line.
夜間、原動機付自転車もヘッドライトを点灯しなければならない。: mopeds must also have headlights on at night.
夜間走行時は、原動機付自転車を含むすべての車両がヘッドライトを点灯する義務がある: All vehicles, including mopeds, are required to turn on their headlights when riding at night.
「進入禁止」の標識は、逆走を防ぐために一方通行の出口側に設置されることが多い。: "No Entry" signs are often placed on the exit side of one-way streets to prevent people from driving the wrong way.
この警戒標識は前方にカーブや幅員減少など速度を落とすべき状況があることを予告して: This warning sign warns you that there is a situation ahead where you should reduce your speed, such as a curve or a narrow road.
二輪車も四輪車と同じ通行方法のルールに従い、右折時は対向直進車・左折車を優先させ: Motorcycles follow the same traffic rules as four-wheeled vehicles, and when making a right turn, they must give priority to oncoming vehicles or vehicles turning left.
専用通行帯は特定の車両（バスなど）専用であり、原付が対象でない場合はその車線を走: Dedicated lanes are reserved for specific vehicles (buses, etc.), and mopeds are not allowed to drive in those lanes.
交差点で「専用通行帯」を示す標示がある場合、原付は必ずその車線を走行しなければな: If there is a sign indicating a "exclusive lane' at an intersection, mopeds must always travel in that lane.
優先道路を走行している自動二輪車は、交差する道路から出てくる車両に対して優先され: Motorcycles traveling on priority roads have priority over vehicles exiting from intersecting roads.
自動二輪車が右折するときは、四輪車と同様に対向車線の直進車・左折車の通行を妨げて: When a motorcycle turns right, it must not obstruct the passage of vehicles traveling straight or turning left in the oncoming lane, just like four-wheeled vehicles.
進入禁止の標識は一方通行道路の出口側に設置され、逆方向からの進入を防ぐ役割を持つ: No entry signs are placed at the exit of one-way roads to prevent entry from the opposite direction.
優先道路を通行する車両は、種類にかかわらず交差する道路から出てくる車両より優先さ: Vehicles traveling on priority roads have priority over vehicles exiting from intersecting roads, regardless of type.
「速度落とせ」の警戒標識は、その先の道路状況に応じて速度を落とすよう促すものであ: The "Slow Down" warning sign urges drivers to slow down depending on the road conditions ahead.
右折待ち車両の陰に二輪車が隠れて見落とされることが多いため、右折車はバイクの巻き: Motorcycles are often hidden behind vehicles waiting to turn right and are overlooked, so right-turning vehicles need to thoroughly check to see if motorcycles are involved.
道幅が明らかに狭い道路から広い道路に進入する場合、広い道路を通行する車両が優先さ: When entering a wider road from an obviously narrow road, vehicles traveling on the wider road have priority, so it is necessary to drive slowly and check for safety.
対向車が右折待ちをしているとき、直進する自動二輪車は対向車の陰に隠れやすいため、: When an oncoming vehicle is waiting to make a right turn, a motorcycle traveling straight can easily hide behind the oncoming vehicle, so oncoming vehicles also need to be careful.
横断歩道を渡っている歩行者がいる場合、自動二輪車は歩行者の通行を妨げないよう一時: If there is a pedestrian crossing the street, the motorcycle must stop temporarily so as not to obstruct the pedestrian's passage.
緊急自動車が接近した場合、バイクを含むすべての車両は道路の左側に寄って一時停止し: If an emergency vehicle approaches, all vehicles, including motorcycles, should stop on the left side of the road and avoid blocking traffic.
サイレンを鳴らして走行する緊急自動車が接近してきたときは、バイクは車と同様に道路: When an emergency vehicle approaches with its siren blaring, the motorcycle must stop on the left side of the road, just like a car.
左折時は巻き込み事故を防ぐため、左折先を直進する自転車や横断する歩行者を優先させ: When making a left turn, you must give priority to cyclists going straight ahead and pedestrians crossing the road in order to prevent accidents.
横断歩道を渡っている、または渡ろうとしている歩行者がいる場合、車両は種類を問わず: If a pedestrian is crossing or attempting to cross at a crosswalk, all vehicles must stop and give the pedestrian priority.
四輪車の運転者は二輪車の存在を軽視せず、常に安全な間隔を保って走行しなければなら: Drivers of four-wheeled vehicles must not ignore the presence of two-wheeled vehicles and must always maintain a safe distance from them.
自動二輪車が走行しているとき、四輪車の運転者は二輪車を軽視して幅寄せしてもよい。: When a motorcycle is running, a driver of a four-wheeled vehicle may disregard the motorcycle and pull alongside.
道幅が明らかに狭い道路から広い道路に進入する自動二輪車は、広い道路の車両より優先: Motorcycles entering a wider road from an obviously narrower road have priority over vehicles on the wider road.
自動二輪車が左折するときは、その左折先の直進する自転車や歩行者よりも優先して通行: When a motorcycle makes a left turn, it has priority over bicycles and pedestrians who are going straight in the direction of the left turn.
路線バスが発進の合図を出しているときは、バイクを含む後続車両はできる限りその進路: When a route bus is giving the signal to start, vehicles following it, including motorcycles, should cooperate and give way to the bus as much as possible.
自転車横断帯を自転車が横断しているときは、車両の種類にかかわらずその通行を妨げな: When a bicycle is crossing the bicycle crossing zone, stop temporarily so as not to obstruct the bicycle, regardless of the type of vehicle.
路線バスが停留所から発進の合図を出しているとき、後続のバイクは道を譲る必要はない: When a route bus signals to start from a stop, motorcycles following it do not have to yield.
交差点で複数の車両が同時に到達し道幅が同じ場合、バイクよりも四輪車が優先される。: When multiple vehicles arrive at an intersection at the same time and the road width is the same, four-wheeled vehicles have priority over motorcycles.
道幅が同じ交差点で同時に到達した場合、車両の種類にかかわらず、左方から来た車両が: If they arrive at an intersection with the same road width at the same time, the vehicle coming from the left will have priority, regardless of the type of vehicle.
自動二輪車は路面電車が走行しているとき、追い越してはならない。: Motorcycles must not overtake streetcars when they are moving.
見通しの悪い交差点に近づくバイクは、優先道路であっても速度を落として周囲を確認す: Motorcyclists approaching intersections with poor visibility should slow down and check their surroundings, even if they have the right of way.
私有地や駐車場から道路に出る車両は、種類にかかわらず道路を走行する車両の通行を妨: Vehicles exiting onto the road from private property or parking lots must not impede the passage of any type of vehicle on the road.
環状交差点に進入する自動二輪車は、環道内をすでに通行している車両より優先される。: Motorcycles entering a roundabout have priority over vehicles already on the ring road.
自転車横断帯を自転車が横断しているときは、自動二輪車も一時停止して待たなければな: When a bicycle is crossing a bicycle crossing zone, motorcycles must also stop and wait.
自動二輪車は、私有地の駐車場から道路に出る際、道路を走行する車両より優先される。: Motorcycles have priority over vehicles on the road when exiting a parking lot on private land.
山道で行き違いが困難な場合、上り坂を走行しているバイクが道を譲るのが原則である。: If it is difficult to pass each other on a mountain road, the general rule is to give way to the motorcycle traveling uphill.
一時停止の標識がある場所では、車両の大きさや周囲の状況にかかわらず必ず停止線の前: Where there is a stop sign, you are obligated to stop in front of the stop line regardless of the size of your vehicle or the surrounding conditions.
右折するバイクは対向直進車に加えて、右折先の横断歩道を渡る歩行者や自転車の通行も: Motorcycles turning right must not obstruct the flow of oncoming vehicles, as well as pedestrians and bicycles crossing the crosswalk where they are turning right.
優先道路であっても、横断歩道を渡る歩行者がいる場合は歩行者が最優先され、車両は一: Even if the road has priority, if there is a pedestrian crossing the road, the pedestrian must have the highest priority and vehicles must stop temporarily.
右折待ちのバイクは、対向車線の直進車だけでなく、横断中の歩行者や自転車にも道を譲: Motorcycles waiting to make a right turn must yield not only to oncoming vehicles traveling straight on, but also to pedestrians and cyclists who are crossing the road.
対向車が右折待ちをしている場合でも、直進車の通行が優先され、右折車はその通過を待: Even if an oncoming vehicle is waiting to turn right, the vehicle going straight ahead has priority, and the vehicle turning right must wait for it to pass.
自動二輪車が交差点を直進するとき、後方から接近する緊急車両にも注意しながら安全確: When a motorcycle goes straight through an intersection, check the safety while paying attention to emergency vehicles approaching from behind.
一時停止の標識がある交差点では、バイクの車両が小さく他に交通がないと見えても必ず: Always stop at intersections with stop signs, even if the motorcycles are small and there is no other traffic.
狭い道で対向車と行き違うとき、自動二輪車は四輪車よりも優先的に道を確保できる。: When passing oncoming vehicles on narrow roads, motorcycles can have priority over four-wheeled vehicles.
交差点内で対向車が右折待ちをしているとき、直進するバイクの通行が優先される。: When an oncoming vehicle is waiting to turn right at an intersection, motorcycles going straight ahead have priority.
狭い道でのすれ違いは車両の種類による優先権はなく、道幅の広い場所を使える側が譲り: When passing each other on a narrow road, there is no right of way depending on the type of vehicle, and the side that can use the wider road yields to each other to pass.
踏切の直前では、バイクも一時停止し、左右の安全を確認してから通過しなければならな: Just before a railroad crossing, motorcyclists must stop temporarily and check that both sides are safe before passing.
自動二輪車は、優先道路上であれば、歩行者の横断があっても速度を落とさず通過してよ: Motorcycles may pass without slowing down even if pedestrians cross the road as long as they are on the priority road.
濡れたマンホールや白線は摩擦係数が低く、その上でハンドル操作をすると滑りやすいた: Wet manholes and white lines have a low coefficient of friction, so be careful when operating the handle on wet manholes or white lines, as they can be slippery.
低速走行時はジャイロ効果（車輪の回転による安定効果）が弱まるため、高速時よりバラ: When driving at low speeds, the gyroscopic effect (stabilizing effect caused by wheel rotation) is weakened, making it easier to lose balance than when driving at high speeds.
カーブ進入前に十分減速しておくことで、カーブ中の急なブレーキ操作による転倒の危険: By slowing down sufficiently before entering a curve, you can reduce the risk of falling due to sudden braking during the curve.
カーブを曲がるときは、進入前にあらかじめ十分に減速し、カーブの中では極力ブレーキ: When turning a curve, slow down sufficiently before entering the curve, and avoid applying the brakes as much as possible during the curve.
カーブでは、車体を傾けるだけでなく、体も車体と一緒に自然に傾けることでスムーズに: When taking a curve, the vehicle not only leans, but also allows the body to naturally lean along with the vehicle, allowing for smooth turns.
交差点通過時は前方だけでなく、後方から接近する緊急車両などにも常に注意を払う必要: When passing through an intersection, you must always pay attention not only to the road ahead, but also to emergency vehicles approaching from behind.
低速で走行しているときは、時速の速いときよりもバランスを崩しやすい。: When driving at low speeds, it is easier to lose balance than when driving at high speeds.
カーブでは車体の傾きに合わせて自然に体を傾けることで、安定したコーナリングができ: In curves, the driver naturally leans his/her body to match the inclination of the vehicle, allowing for stable cornering.
曲がる方向に視線を向けることで、車体が自然にその方向へ安定して曲がりやすくなる。: By directing your gaze in the direction you are turning, your vehicle will naturally turn in that direction more stably and more easily.
急な下り坂でバイクの速度を落とすときは、前輪ブレーキだけを強くかけるのが最も安全: When slowing your bike down a steep slope, it's safest to apply only the front brake.
カーブを曲がるときは、視線をカーブの出口や曲がっていく先の方向に向けるとバランス: When turning a curve, it is easier to maintain balance if you direct your eyes toward the exit of the curve or the direction in which you are turning.
雨で濡れたマンホールや白線の上でハンドルを大きく切ると、スリップしやすい。: If you turn the steering wheel sharply over a wet manhole or white line, you are likely to slip.
二輪車は四輪車と違い、停止時や低速時に自ら重心バランスを保つ必要がある。: Unlike four-wheeled vehicles, two-wheeled vehicles must maintain their own center of gravity balance when stopped or at low speeds.
砂利道やぬかるみでは、急なハンドル操作や急ブレーキを避け、車体をまっすぐ保つよう: On gravel roads or muddy roads, avoid sudden steering or sudden braking, and try to keep the vehicle straight.
バイクを停止させるときは、両足を地面につけて安定させてから完全に停止するのが基本: When stopping a bike, it is important to put both feet on the ground for stability and then come to a complete stop.
停止時には両足を地面につけてバランスをとることで、低速時や停止時の転倒を防ぐこと: By placing both feet on the ground when stopping for balance, you can prevent falls at low speeds or when stopping.
カーブを曲がる際、路面の中央にある油や砂は特に気にする必要はない。: When taking a curve, there is no need to worry about oil or sand in the center of the road.
連続するカーブが続く山道では、カーブごとにアクセルとブレーキのタイミングを一定に: On mountain roads with continuous curves, there is no need to adjust the timing of accelerator and brake at the same time for each curve.
カーブに進入する速度が速すぎると感じた場合、カーブの途中で強くブレーキをかけて速: If you feel that you are entering a curve too fast, it is a good idea to brake hard midway through the curve to adjust your speed.
カーブの路面にある油や砂はタイヤのグリップを著しく低下させるため、その上を通らな: Oil and sand on curved roads can significantly reduce tire grip, so care must be taken not to drive over them.
急な下り坂では前輪ブレーキのみを強くかけると転倒の危険があるため、前後輪のブレー: When going down a steep slope, there is a risk of falling if only the front brakes are applied strongly, so use the front and rear brakes in a balanced manner.
カーブの途中で急ブレーキをかけると転倒の危険が高いため、進入前に十分減速しておく: If you brake suddenly in the middle of a curve, there is a high risk of falling, so it is important to slow down sufficiently before entering the curve.
不安定な路面では急な操作が転倒を招くため、車体をまっすぐに保ちながらゆっくりと通: On unstable roads, sudden maneuvers can cause the vehicle to fall, so it is important to keep the vehicle straight and drive slowly.
連続するカーブでは、それぞれのカーブの曲率や見通しに応じて速度とブレーキのタイミ: Successive curves require adjusting speed and braking timing depending on the curvature and visibility of each curve.
交差点を曲がるときは、歩行者や自転車との事故を防ぐため、車体をあまり傾けず低速で: When turning at an intersection, you should carefully turn at a low speed without leaning your vehicle too much to prevent accidents with pedestrians or bicycles.
強い横風を受けたときは、ハンドルを固く握りしめ、風上側に体を傾けてバランスをとる: When you are caught in a strong crosswind, grip the handle firmly and lean your body upwind to maintain balance.
轍にタイヤがとられるとハンドル操作が不安定になるため、轍を避けるか慎重に走行する: If the tires get caught in the ruts, the steering becomes unstable, so it is necessary to avoid the ruts or drive carefully.
轍（わだち）がある道路を走行するときは、轍にタイヤをとられないよう注意しながら走: When driving on a road with ruts, be careful not to get your tires caught in the ruts.
バイクの重心は高い位置に荷物を積むほど不安定になるため、できるだけ低い位置に積む: The higher the bike's center of gravity is loaded, the more unstable it becomes, so it's best to load it as low as possible.
狭いUターンではハンドルを大きく切り、視線を進行方向に向けることでバランスを保ち: When making a narrow U-turn, it is easier to maintain balance by turning the steering wheel sharply and directing your eyes toward the direction of travel.
交差点を曲がるときは、車体を大きく傾けて速いスピードで曲がることが推奨される。: When turning at an intersection, it is recommended to lean the vehicle significantly and turn at high speed.
バイクの重心は、荷物を高い位置に積むほど安定しやすくなる。: The center of gravity of the bike becomes more stable the higher you load your luggage.
カーブでの適切な速度は路面状況や視界、カーブの曲率によって異なり、常に慎重に判断: The appropriate speed for a curve depends on road conditions, visibility, and the curvature of the curve, so careful judgment is always necessary.
カーブでの適切な速度は、乾いた路面であれば天候にかかわらず常に一定でよい。: The appropriate speed for a curve should always be the same on a dry road, regardless of the weather.
狭いスペースでUターンをするときは、ハンドルを大きく切り、視線を進みたい方向に向: When making a U-turn in a tight space, it's a good idea to turn the steering wheel wide and direct your eyes in the direction you want to go.
二輪車は自立しないため、停止時や低速時は運転者自身がバランスを取り続ける必要があ: Motorcycles do not stand on their own, so the driver must maintain balance when stopped or at low speeds.
大型トラックの直後は運転手から見えない死角になりやすいため、車間距離を十分にとっ: Drivers tend to have a blind spot right behind a large truck, so it is necessary to maintain a sufficient distance between vehicles.
車体が傾いた状態でブレーキをかけるとタイヤのグリップを失いやすいため、直立姿勢で: If you apply the brakes when the vehicle is tilted, the tires tend to lose grip, so it is best to decelerate while standing upright.
濡れたマンホールの上で車体を傾けると滑りやすいため、できるだけ車体を起こした状態: If you tilt your vehicle over a wet manhole, it will be slippery, so you should cross it with your vehicle as upright as possible.
横風を受けたときは、風上側にわずかに体を傾けることで、風によるふらつきを抑えバラ: When you are hit by a crosswind, tilting your body slightly to the windward side will prevent you from being swayed by the wind and help you maintain your balance.
低速バランス走行の練習を重ねることで、実際の道路でのふらつきや転倒を防ぐ運転技術: By repeatedly practicing low-speed balanced driving, you will acquire driving techniques that will prevent you from swaying and falling on actual roads.
急な加減速はタイヤのグリップを不安定にし、バイクのバランスを崩して転倒につながる: Sudden acceleration and deceleration can make the tires' grip unstable, causing you to lose your bike's balance and cause you to fall.
カーブの手前で速度を落とす際は、車体を起こした状態（直立姿勢）でブレーキをかける: When slowing down before a curve, the basic rule is to apply the brakes while the vehicle is in an upright position.
大型トラックの後方を走行するときは、トラックの死角に入らないよう、車間距離を十分: When driving behind a large truck, maintain sufficient distance between vehicles to avoid walking into the truck's blind spot.
四輪車の運転者からバイクは見えやすいため、二輪車側が特に注意する必要はない。: Motorcycles are easily visible to four-wheeled vehicle drivers, so two-wheeled vehicles do not need to be particularly careful.
低速でのUターンやスラローム走行の練習は、バランス感覚の向上に役立つ。: Practicing U-turns and slalom runs at low speeds will help improve your sense of balance.
急発進や急停止を繰り返す運転は、バイクの安定性を損ない転倒のリスクを高める。: Riding with repeated sudden starts and stops impairs the stability of the bike and increases the risk of falling.
雨の日にマンホールの上を通過するときは、車体を傾けたまま通過しても問題ない。: When passing over a manhole on a rainy day, there is no problem even if the vehicle is tilted.
右折待ちの車の運転者は対向直進車を確認する際、車体の小さいバイクを見落としやすい: Drivers of vehicles waiting to make a right turn may easily overlook small-bodied motorcycles when checking for oncoming vehicles, so motorcyclists should also be careful about their speed.
バイクは車体が小さいため四輪車のドライバーから見落とされやすく、二輪車側が特に注: Because motorcycles are small, they are easily overlooked by drivers of four-wheeled vehicles, so motorcyclists must be especially careful and drive defensively.
四輪車の横に並走し続けると相手の死角に入り続けることになるため、追い越すか十分に: If you continue to drive alongside a four-wheeler, you will continue to be in the other party's blind spot, so you should either overtake or drive at a sufficient distance.
